upstream_context.intent: Expand baseline controls with safety-lifecycle, secure coding, and evidence-strength directives

directives.title: Fail-Closed Normative Interpretation
directives.rationale: High-assurance prompt pipelines must preserve strict normative semantics across rewriting and summarization.
implementation_patterns: Normalize and preserve RFC 2119/8174 keyword meaning before rule execution.
Convert ambiguous wording into explicit MUST/SHOULD form only with traceable justification.
Reject outputs that remove prohibitions or weaken hard requirements.
verification_checks: Normative token audit shows no MUST->SHOULD downgrade.
Requirement-level metadata survives each transformation stage.
Ambiguous clauses are flagged for explicit owner decision.

directives.title: Assurance Crosswalk Enforcement
directives.rationale: AES maps risk to assurance rigor; crosswalk to aerospace/automotive/functional-safety standards prevents under-classification.
implementation_patterns: Classify each change by impact severity before implementation.
For high-impact changes, require lifecycle artifacts comparable to DO-178C/ISO 26262/IEC 61508 expectations.
Attach traceability from requirement -> implementation -> test evidence.
verification_checks: Assurance class exists for every executable change.
High-assurance changes include bidirectional traceability evidence.
Missing classification blocks merge.

directives.title: Security and Weakness Baseline with Drift Watch
directives.rationale: Security checks must remain aligned to ASVS and CWE while handling annual taxonomy changes without silent policy drift.
implementation_patterns: Enforce ASVS-level security checklists by component exposure.
Treat relevant CWE Top 25 classes as blocking for high-assurance surfaces.
On new CWE/ASVS releases, open policy-delta review tickets before changing enforcement.
verification_checks: Checklist evidence links to ASVS sections or mapped internal controls.
Blocking CWE findings require mitigation or approved exception.
Upstream release delta exists when baseline versions change.

directives.title: NIST-Aligned Control Family Coverage
directives.rationale: Control-family mapping makes security intent auditable and comparable across services.
implementation_patterns: Map high-risk requirements to SP 800-53 families (minimum AC/AU/CM/SC).
Store mappings as machine-readable evidence artifacts.
Require control rationale for omitted families in scoped exceptions.
verification_checks: Every high-risk requirement has at least one control-family tag.
Audit export includes control coverage table.
Unjustified missing control family is a hard failure.

directives.title: Safe-Subset Coding for Native Components
directives.rationale: High-assurance native code benefits from constrained language subsets and explicit undefined-behavior avoidance.
implementation_patterns: Apply CERT C/C++ secure coding constraints for integer, memory, and API safety.
Apply MISRA-aligned restrictions for dangerous casts and undefined behavior patterns.
Require explicit concurrency and resource-ownership strategy in design notes.
verification_checks: Static analysis profile includes CERT/MISRA-aligned rule packs.
Undefined-behavior risk items are enumerated and resolved.
Unsafe cast or unchecked arithmetic in critical paths is blocking.

directives.title: Supply-Chain Provenance and Attestation
directives.rationale: High-assurance releases require provenance that is attributable, tamper-evident, and reviewable.
implementation_patterns: Target SLSA Level 3 provenance characteristics for critical artifact pipelines.
Record immutable source, build parameters, and builder identity in attestations.
Require signing/verification step before high-assurance release publication.
verification_checks: Attestation attached to each release artifact.
Provenance links reconstruct complete build lineage.
Unsigned or unverifiable critical artifact is blocked.

directives.title: Telemetry Semantics and Correlation Rigor
directives.rationale: Consistent semantic conventions and correlation IDs are required for actionable reliability gates.
implementation_patterns: Emit logs/metrics/traces with a pinned semantic-convention version.
Carry trace/span identifiers across process and service boundaries.
Use histogram-style latency metrics for p95/p99 regressions.
verification_checks: Telemetry schema validation passes against selected semantic conventions.
Cross-signal correlation works for a representative transaction.
Latency gate can compute percentile regressions from emitted metrics.

directives.title: Evidence-Backed Quantum/Physics Claims
directives.rationale: When standards are incomplete, high-risk numerical or quantum claims require reproducible evidence tied to peer-reviewed literature.
implementation_patterns: Require explicit noise-model quality measurement before claiming robustness gains.
Require A/B evidence for noise-adaptive transpilation impact claims.
For conservation-law-sensitive numerics, require entropy/stability rationale and tests.
verification_checks: Claimed quantum robustness includes baseline-vs-treatment metrics.
Noise model assumptions are documented and empirically validated.
Numerical stability claims include reproducible test artifacts.

directives.title: Governed Baseline Evolution
directives.rationale: External standards evolve; controlled baseline updates prevent accidental weakening or incompatible policy jumps.
implementation_patterns: Run periodic upstream change scans for ASVS/CWE/NIST/SLSA/OpenTelemetry.
Create migration tickets before any baseline version change.
Require multi-owner signoff and impact report before activation.
verification_checks: Each baseline bump includes signed impact analysis.
Rule delta table exists and is linked to migration tasks.
No active policy references unresolved baseline transition.
